questions: You don't get self-conscious about social mishaps. You trip and fall in front of a crowded cafe patio while people are watching. How do you react?
You don't get self-conscious about social mishaps. You accidentally call your boss by the wrong name during a presentation in front of the whole department. What do you feel?
You don't get self-conscious about social mishaps. You wave enthusiastically at someone in a store who you thought was your friend, but it's a total stranger. What goes through your mind?

You don't get self-conscious about social mishaps. You realize mid-conversation at a party that you have spinach stuck in your teeth and have been smiling at everyone for the past hour. How do you handle it?
You don't get self-conscious about social mishaps. Your phone starts playing loud music in a silent waiting room and you fumble to turn it off while everyone stares. How do you feel?
You don't get self-conscious about social mishaps. You confidently give a wrong answer in a meeting and your colleague politely corrects you in front of everyone. What's your internal response?
You don't get self-conscious about social mishaps. You show up to a casual house party wearing formal clothes because you misread the invitation. How do you deal with it?
You don't get self-conscious about social mishaps. You accidentally send a text complaining about a coworker to that coworker instead of your friend. What do you do?
You don't get self-conscious about social mishaps. You tell a joke at a dinner table that completely falls flat and everyone just stares at you in silence. How do you feel?
You don't get self-conscious about social mishaps. You walk into a glass door at an office building while the receptionist and several visitors are watching. What's your reaction?
